title: MSPs to vote on banning US troops from Scottish airports

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: US military flights have landed at Prestwick Airport over 550 times since April 1 2025 (Image: Ben Birchall/PA) MSPs are to vote on whether to evict US military forces from Prestwick Airport, following continued military activity and reports of breaches of Scottish jurisdiction. The Scottish Greens are to use a parliamentary debate on Wednesday to call on the US military to be banned from using Prestwick and other publicly owned airports in Scotland.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: New data compiled by the Scottish Greens using Flightradar24 shows that US military flights have landed at Prestwick Airport more than 550 times since April 1 2025, with activity occurring on most days. This follows an escalation of US president Donald Trump ’s aggression on the world stage, with recent reports that the US ignored a decision of the Court of Session and removed two individuals from Scottish waters in January .

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: READ MORE: Yvette Cooper defends decision to ban Palestine Action in wake of court ruling Scottish Green co-leader Gillian Mackay MSP has argued that the amount the airport is being used “is not just the occasional stop-off" and is "sustained and routine military use of publicly-owned Scottish infrastructure". She has called on the Scottish Government to vote with her party on Wednesday, saying ministers "must act decisively".

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – MSPs to vote on banning Donald Trump's troops from using Scottish airports
